subject: Social and Political Life - II
chapter_name: Growing Up as Boys and Girls
overall_learning_objectives: Analyze how social identities are formed through gender-specific upbringing in different cultures
Distinguish between the societal valuation of 'productive' work and 'reproductive' (domestic) work
Identify the physical and emotional labor involved in housework and care-giving
Explain the socio-economic challenges faced by domestic workers
Understand the concept of 'Double Burden' in the context of working women
Evaluate government interventions aimed at bridging the gender gap in labor and education
Relate constitutional provisions of equality to everyday gender practices

lesson_plan.title: Cross-Cultural Perspectives on Gender
topics_covered: Samoan Islands (1920s): A non-formal education model
Madhya Pradesh (1960s): Gendered use of public spaces
Societal 'marking' of boys and girls
teaching_strategy: Comparative study: Samoan flexibility vs. MP rigidity
Spatial analysis: The street as a 'playground' (boys) vs. a 'passage' (girls)
Discussion: How environment shapes gender behavior
student_activities: Create a Venn diagram comparing the childhood of a Samoan boy/girl with an Indian child
Analyze the 'purpose of the street' for boys and girls in their own locality
assessment: Short response: 'How did Samoan society view childcare in the 1920s?'
Identify the specific gender barriers in the 1960s MP school system

lesson_plan.title: Socialization and Gendered Toys
topics_covered: The role of toys and games in preparing children for future roles
Gender as a social construct rather than just a biological difference
The impact of 'Soft skills' vs. 'Hard skills' expectations
teaching_strategy: Inquiry-based learning: 'Why aren't boys encouraged to play with dolls?'
Linking childhood play to adult career segregation
Visual analysis: Advertisements for children's toys
student_activities: Deconstruct a toy advertisement and identify which gender it targets and why
Debate: 'Do toys influence our choice of profession in adulthood?'
assessment: Explanation of the term 'Socialization' in the context of gender
Quiz on gender stereotypes in play

lesson_plan.title: The Invisible Labor: Housework
topics_covered: The story of Hermeet's family: Challenging the 'Mother doesn't work' myth
Devaluation of domestic work
Characteristics of housework: Invisible, physically demanding, and time-consuming
teaching_strategy: Analyzing the comic strip: Identifying the 'Leisure myth'
Time-use survey analysis: Comparing working hours of men and women
Discussion: Why is domestic work not considered 'Real Work'?
student_activities: Conduct a 24-hour time-log for a female family member and calculate her 'working hours'
List tasks involved in 'Care-giving' and their emotional value
assessment: Essay: 'Why is the work done at home invisible to society?'

lesson_plan.title: Marginalized Labor: Domestic Workers
topics_covered: The life and struggles of Melani: A case study
Low wages and lack of dignity in domestic help
The physical toll of cleaning, washing, and cooking
teaching_strategy: Reading and reflecting on Melani’s narrative
Discussion on the lack of labor laws for domestic workers
Analyzing the relationship between class and gender in domestic labor
student_activities: Write a fictional interview with a domestic worker about their daily challenges
Discuss: 'Why does society pay so little for tasks that are so essential?'
assessment: Matching: Work type vs. Physical effort required
Short answer: 'Explain the term 'Care-giving' using examples'

lesson_plan.title: The Double Burden and Constitutional Rights
topics_covered: Concept of 'Double Burden'
Constitutional mandate: Article 15 and Gender Equality
The State's responsibility in rectifying gender imbalance
teaching_strategy: Diagrammatic representation of the Double Burden
Analyzing why individual families alone cannot solve inequality
Discussion on the difference between 'Equality' and 'Equity'
student_activities: Identify local examples where women face a double burden (e.g., teaching and cooking)
Research the 1996 Supreme Court guidelines on workplace safety for women (Basics)
assessment: Identification of the Constitutional provision for equality

lesson_plan.title: Policy Interventions: Crèches and Anganwadis
topics_covered: Provisions for Anganwadis and Childcare centres
Mandatory Crèche laws for organizations with more than 30 women employees
Impact of these laws on girls' education and women's employment
teaching_strategy: Evaluating the success of the Anganwadi system in rural India
Logical deduction: How a crèche helps an older sister attend school
Discussion on 'Institutional Support' vs. 'Individual Struggle'
student_activities: Draft a letter to a factory owner explaining why they should set up a crèche
Identify the functions of an Anganwadi worker in your community
assessment: Matching: Government scheme vs. Intended benefit
Short response: 'How do crèches promote girls' education?'

lesson_plan.title: Review and Mastery Synthesis
topics_covered: Synthesis of Samoa, MP, and Modern India
Valuing domestic labor and dignity
NCERT exercise mastery
teaching_strategy: Concept mapping: Gender socialization $ightarrow$ Devaluation $ightarrow$ Inequality $ightarrow$ Policy
Peer-led discussion on 'The future of gender roles'
Solving Assertion-Reasoning questions
student_activities: Complete the full chapter mind map
Final Unit Test and Feedback session

teaching_aids: NCERT Social and Political Life - II Textbook
Video snippets of domestic labor conditions
teacher_tip: At the medium level, it is important to challenge the 'naturalness' of gender roles. Use the Samoan example to show that what we consider 'natural' for boys or girls is often just a cultural choice. This helps students look at their own lives with a critical eye.
